title: zoneStat allocates three separate number arrays per zone and sorts all three, iterating the full pixel buffer once per zone call (three calls total = three full passes).
description: For a 1200×(~900) comparison image (~1.08 million pixels), `zoneStat` is called three times. Each call iterates all pixels once (O(n)) and then sorts the per-zone arrays. Total iterations: ~3.24M pixel reads plus three sort operations. A single-pass implementation that bins pixels into all three zones simultaneously would reduce pixel reads by 3x and is straightforward. For a dev diagnostic script this is not a blocker, but analysis of a 20 MP source at full resolution would be noticeably slow. Flagged because it could matter if `CMP_W` is increased for finer analysis.

title: Full-resolution RGB buffer (~50 MB for a 20 MP ORF) is taken from WASM and held in JS memory before downscaling; peak RSS will be high if called repeatedly.
description: The pipeline produces a full-resolution RGB16 result. `result.take_rgb()` moves it to JS. The JS-side `downscale_rgb` then reduces it to 1200×N. For a single run this is fine, but the peak memory usage includes the full-res buffer plus the downscaled copy simultaneously. Since `take_rgb` is a move (not a copy) from WASM memory, the WASM side is freed, but on the JS side both `fullRgb` and `oursRgb` exist concurrently until GC. For a dev script run once, this is not a problem. Flagged only because a user increasing `CMP_W` to full resolution would be surprised by the memory usage.
